reasoning: The Lean formalization correctly encodes the collection $\mathcal{T}_\infty$: "X - U is infinite" becomes `Set.Infinite tᶜ`, "X - U is empty" becomes `t = Set.univ`, and "X - U is all of X" becomes `t = ∅`. The three negated conditions (univ ∈ T_inf, closure under arbitrary sUnion, closure under pairwise intersection) are exactly the three axioms of a topology in Mathlib's `TopologicalSpace` class; note that `∅ ∈ T_inf` follows from the sUnion condition applied to the empty collection, so these three are equivalent to "T_inf is a topology". The "does not need to be a topology" phrasing is captured by the existential quantifier `∃ X : Type` asserting that some type X witnesses the failure, which is the standard reading. The encoding is logically equivalent to the informal claim.
